from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase (more concise for common predicates)
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "was started in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is in",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "leader is",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to avoid repeating the subject in every clause.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        parts = []
        handled = set()

        # Helper to clean quoted objects
        def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
            return o.strip('"').strip("'")

        # Special predicates handling for more natural phrasing
        for tr in group:
            pred = tr.predicate
            obj = clean_obj(tr.object)

            if pred == "birthPlace":
                parts.append(f"{subject} was born in {obj}")
                handled.add(pred)
            elif pred == "deathPlace":
                parts.append(f"{subject} died in {obj}")
                handled.add(pred)
            elif pred == "language":
                # Remove redundant “language” suffix if present
                if obj.lower().endswith(" language"):
                    obj = obj[: -len(" language")]
                parts.append(f"One of the languages in {subject} is {obj}")
                handled.add(pred)
            elif pred == "ethnicGroup":
                parts.append(f"One of the ethnic groups in {subject} is {obj}")
                handled.add(pred)
            elif pred == "capital":
                parts.append(f"The capital of {subject} is {obj}")
                handled.add(pred)
            elif pred == "leaderTitle":
                parts.append(f"The leader title of {subject} is {obj}")
                handled.add(pred)
            elif pred == "demonym":
                parts.append(f"The demonym of {subject} is {obj}")
                handled.add(pred)
            elif pred == "buildingStartDate":
                parts.append(f"Construction of {subject} began in {obj}")
                handled.add(pred)
            elif pred == "inaugurationDate":
                parts.append(f"The inauguration of {subject} was on {obj}")
                handled.add(pred)
            elif pred == "cost":
                # Natural phrasing for cost (object‑first)
                parts.append(f"{obj} is the cost to build {subject}")
                handled.add(pred)
            elif pred == "leader":
                # Natural phrasing for leader (object‑first)
                parts.append(f"{obj} is the leader of {subject}")
                handled.add(pred)
            elif pred == "height":
                # Natural phrasing for height
                height_obj = obj.replace(" (metres)", " metres")
                parts.append(f"The height of {subject} is {height_obj}")
                handled.add(pred)
            elif pred == "architect":
                # Active voice: <person> designed <building>
                parts.append(f"{obj} designed {subject}")
                handled.add(pred)
            elif pred == "significantBuilding":
                # Active voice for person -> building relationship
                parts.append(f"{subject} designed {obj}")
                handled.add(pred)

        # Preferred ordering of common attributes for readability.
        order = ["location", "height", "architect", "buildingType", "completionDate"]
        for pred in order:
            if pred in handled:
                continue
            for tr in group:
                if tr.predicate == pred:
                    verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
                    clause = f"{verb} {clean_obj(tr.object)}"
                    parts.append(clause)
                    handled.add(pred)
                    break

        # Add remaining predicates that were not handled above.
        for tr in group:
            if tr.predicate in handled:
                continue
            verb = verb_map.get(tr.predicate, "has")
            obj = clean_obj(tr.object)

            # Special handling for count predicates to use appropriate nouns
            if tr.predicate == "floorCount":
                clause = f"{verb} {obj} floors"
            elif tr.predicate == "bedCount":
                clause = f"{verb} {obj} beds"
            elif tr.predicate == "numberOfRooms":
                clause = f"{verb} {obj} rooms"
            # Special handling for country to add article when appropriate
            elif tr.predicate == "country":
                # Add "the" for country names that conventionally take an article
                if any(keyword in obj for keyword in ["States", "Kingdom", "Republic", "Union", "Islands", "Federated"]):
                    clause = f"{verb} the {obj}"
                else:
                    clause = f"{verb} {obj}"
            else:
                clause = f"{verb} {obj}"
            parts.append(clause)

        # Build the sentence for this subject.
        if parts:
            # If the first clause already starts with a definite article (e.g., “The height …”), avoid repeating the subject.
            first_part = parts[0]
            if first_part.lower().startswith("the "):
                sentence = ", ".join(parts) + "."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts) + "."
        else:
            sentence = subject + "."

        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine sentences for multiple subjects.
    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    # Capitalize the first character of the whole output.
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
